cloud.title: Discover from Hisense app
cloud.description: Enter the app code, username, and password for the mobile app account where your air conditioner is paired. For choosing the correct app code, see [Supported App Codes](https://github.com/cvladan/hassio-hacs-hisense-aircon#supported-app-codes). Advanced Settings are optional and can stay collapsed for most installs.

data_description.app: Choose the code for the mobile app where your air conditioner is paired. See [Supported App Codes](https://github.com/cvladan/hassio-hacs-hisense-aircon#supported-app-codes).

advanced_settings.name: Advanced Settings
advanced_settings.description: Most users should leave these values unchanged. Use this section only if you need to add one specific device from the account, if Home Assistant has multiple network interfaces or VLANs, if your Home Assistant HTTP port is not 8123, or if you want to change how often the integration asks the device for a full property refresh.

data_description.device_name: Optional exact device name from the mobile app. Leave empty to add every compatible device found in this app account.
data_description.local_ip: Optional IP address that Home Assistant should advertise to the air conditioner. Leave empty for automatic detection. Set this when Home Assistant has multiple IP addresses, Docker/macvlan networking, or VLAN routing and the device cannot reach the automatically chosen address.
data_description.callback_port: Plain HTTP port on Home Assistant that the air conditioner can reach. This is usually 8123. Change it only if Home Assistant is exposed to the device on a different local HTTP port.
data_description.status_interval: How often, in seconds, Home Assistant queues a full property refresh for the device. Lower values update stale entities sooner but create more local traffic; higher values are quieter but slower to notice changes made outside Home Assistant.
data_description.temp_type: Use auto unless temperatures or valid ranges look wrong. Auto uses the selected app code; if the app does not identify a native unit, Home Assistant's configured temperature unit is used as the fallback.

error.cannot_connect: Could not connect to the Hisense/Ayla service or the credentials were rejected.
error.invalid_manual_config: Manual configuration is incomplete or invalid.

data_description.local_ip: Use only when Home Assistant has multiple network interfaces or VLANs. Leave empty for automatic detection.
data_description.callback_port: The plain HTTP port that the air conditioner can reach, usually 8123.
data_description.status_interval: How often Home Assistant queues a full property refresh, in seconds.
data_description.temp_type: Use auto unless temperatures or valid ranges look wrong. Auto keeps the unit detected during setup; C or F overrides the stored device unit after the integration reloads.
